observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:
 
(a) require the Municipal Budget Officer together with the focal person and focal departments/offices to continue appropriating at least 5% for GAD-related PPAs in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum No. 2013-01; and 

(b) direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to submit to the Audit Team the GAD AR within five working days from the end of January of the ensuing year.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MAO and MTO to closely
coordinate in reconciling the noted difference of ₱2,513,190.42 and reconcile their
respective cash record balances thereafter, regularly and (b) MT to submit to the
MAO a list of unclaimed/unreleased checks in her possession every end of the year;
and (c) MA to draw the necessary adjusting entries for the reversion/restoration to
the Cash-in-Bank account and appropriate liability/payable accounts the total
amount of unreleased checks to reflect the correct account balances in the year-end
books of account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit copies of Bank Reconciliation Statements and its
supporting documents such as Bank Statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and
paid checks and other documents necessary to facilitate the timely verification of the
Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to (a)
recognize impairment allowance of ₱525,929.33 on the dormant and unsubstantiated
accounts pending the filing of the request for write-off; (b) continue to review and
analyze the dormant accounts and prepare a formal request to the COA Audit Team
Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) for the writing-off of the accounts
supported with evidence that all efforts were put forth to determine the details of the
accounts and to recover the receivable accounts; and (c) maintain complete
subsidiary records to prevent the occurrence of dormant and unsubstantiated
accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE (a) create an Inventory
Committee (IC) that will execute the physical inventory of the PPEs owned by the
Municipality and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team; (b) require the Municipal
Accountant and the Property Custodian to (i) reconcile their records with the results
of physical inventory of PPE upon completion and preparation of the report by the
IC; and (ii) maintain updated property records.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to
continuously improve taxes collection through conduct of tax campaigns.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to ensure optimal utilization of the
20% DF by monitoring the full implementation of the projects as embodied in their
local development plan; (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) observe strictly the posting of
signboards at the project sites for public information and publicity in accordance
with provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-001 dated January 30, 2013; and (ii)
complete the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard
Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (c) Municipal Accountant to
review the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects
prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to (a)
submit to the Audit Team the lacking documentary requirements on various COVID-
19 related disbursements; and (b) ensure completeness of documentation prior to
payment of claims against government funds.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) creation of the Municipality’s
website in compliance with the posting requirements of various reports thereon; (b)
Local Finance Committee to (i) prepare the Report on Fund Utilization and Status of
Implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format (Annex B); and (ii) submit said
report, together with the list of beneficiaries to the DBM and Office of the Auditor;
and (c) Municipal Public Information Office to comply with the mandatory posting
of the Report on Fund Utilization of LGSF-FA in the Municipality’s website and in
at least three (3) conspicuous places within the Municipality pursuant to DBM LBC
No. 136 dated March 30, 2021.

We recommended that the LCE require the Human Resource Management
Officer to submit the Office Order/Authority and Accomplishment Reports of the
personnel listed in the payroll to physically report to work during the Enhanced
Community Quarantine (ECQ) as members of the Skeletal Work Force.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Budget Officer
together with the focal person and focal departments/offices to continue
appropriating at least 5% for GAD-related PPAs in accordance with PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA Joint Memorandum No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 or the Guidelines on
the Localization on the Magna Carta of Women; and (b) direct the GAD Focal Point
System (GFPS) to submit to the Audit Team the GAD AR within five working days
from the end of January of the ensuing year.

We recommended that the LCE and the Municipal Environment and Natural
Resources Office (MENRO) (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste
management by activating the Solid Waste Management Committees for the strict
enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws
especially on establishment of MRF and waste segregation at source; (b) intensify
information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on
the necessity of the construction of MRF in every barangay and establishing
programs on composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated; (c)
consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of the five
barangays which remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003
in establishing their own functional MRF; and (d) develop waste reduction programs
such as composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated in order
to reduce hauling and disposal costs of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to comply with the remittance of taxes withheld.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the regular remittance of
premiums and loan amortizations including the government share.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with regulations on the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations; and (b) secure the
HDMF membership number of Job Order personnel concerned to facilitate its
immediate remittance to the HDMF.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the regulations on the
withholding and remittance of monthly contributions and loan amortizations within
the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) liable persons to refund the
disallowance that became final and executory there being no appeal filed within the
prescribed period; and (b) officials involved in the processing of claims to be more
diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions and
disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to: 

(a) prepare a formal request to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) for the writing-off of the accounts supported with evidence that all efforts were put forth to determine the details of the accounts and recover from the receivable accounts; and 

(b) maintain complete subsidiary records to prevent the occurrence of dormant and unsubstantiated accounts.

We recommended that the LCE:
 
(a) considering the impact of COVID19 on the income of the Municipality, exercise prudence in the use of funds so that priority programs, projects and activities can be delivered to constituents; and 

(b) exert efforts to improve the Municipality’s income for continuous delivery of basic services despite the pandemic.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
 
(a) Municipal Engineer to (i) observe strictly the posting of signboards at  the project sites for public information and publicity in accordance with provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-001; (ii) complete the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (iii)  require the respective contractors to repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to  the one year warranty from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184; 

(b) Municipal Planning Officer and the Municipal Engineer to implement and monitor regularly the status of all implemented projects as embodied in their local development plan; and 

(c)  Municipal Accountant to review the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
 
(a) Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documentary requirements on various disbursements made; and 

(b) the General Services Offices and Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the posting and reporting requirements prescribed under DBM LBC No. 125.

We recommended that the LCE require the Human Resource Management Officer to submit the Office Order/Authority and Accomplishment Reports of the personnel listed in the payroll to physically report to work during the Enhanced Community Quarantine (ECQ) as member of the Skeletal Work Force.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply with on the remittance of taxes withheld intact.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within regularly.

We recommended that the LCE require the: 

(a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with regulations on the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations regularly; and 

(b) secure the HDMF membership number of Job Order concerned to facilitate the immediate remittance thereof to the HDMF.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the:

(a) liable persons to refund the disallowance that became final and executory there being no appeal filed within the prescribed period; and 

(b) officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant to (a) prepare a formal request to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) for the writing-off of the accounts supported with evidence that all efforts were put forth to determine the details of the accounts and recover from the receivable accounts; and (b) maintain complete subsidiary records to prevent the occurrence of dormant and unsubstantiated accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE (a) create an Inventory Committee (IC) that will execute the physical inventory of the PPEs owned by the Municipality and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team; (b) require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to (i) reconcile their records with the results of physical inventory of PPE upon completion and preparation of the report by the IC; and (ii) maintain updated property records.

We recommended that the LCE (a) considering the impact of COVID19 on the income of the Municipality, exercise prudence in the use of funds so that priority programs, projects and activities can be delivered to constituents; and (b) exert efforts  to improve the Municipality’s income for  continuous delivery of basic services despite the pandemic.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) Municipal Engineer to (i) observe strictly the posting of signboards at  the project sites for public information and publicity in accordance with provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-001; (ii) complete the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (iii)  require the respective contractors to repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to the one year warranty from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184; (b) Municipal Planning Officer and the Municipal Engineer to implement and monitor regularly the status of all implemented projects as embodied in their local development plan; and (c)  Municipal Accountant to review the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Accountant  to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documentary requirements on various disbursements made; and(b) the General Services Offices and Bids and Awards Committee to  comply with the posting and reporting requirements prescribed under DBM LBC No. 125.

We recommended that the LCE require the Human Resource Management Officer to submit the Office Order/Authority and Accomplishment Reports of the personnel listed in the payroll to physically report to work during the Enhanced Community Quarantine (ECQ) as member of the Skeletal Work Force.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Budget Officer together   with the focal person and focal departments/offices to continue appropriating at least 5% for GAD-related PPAs in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum No. 2013-01; and (b) direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to submit to the Audit Team the GAD AR within five working days from the end of January of the ensuing year.

We recommended that the LCE and the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste management and activate the Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws especially on establishment of MRF and waste segregation at source; (b) intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of   the construction of MRF in every barangay and establishing programs on composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated; (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of the ten barangays which  remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003; and  (d) develop waste reduction programs such as composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated in order to reduce hauling and disposal costs of the Municipality.
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply with  on the remittance of taxes withheld intact.

We recommended that the LCE  require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within regularly.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with regulations on the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations regularly; and (b) secure the HDMF membership number of Job Order  concerned to facilitate the immediate remittance thereof to the HDMF.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) liable persons to refund the disallowance that became final and executory there being no appeal filed within the prescribed period; and (b) officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.
require the Municipal Accountant to remit to the source agencies or to the Bureau of the Treasury the remaining unexpended balance of the trust funds totaling to ₱142,841.40.

(a) require the LDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF certified correct by the Local Accountant and submit to the AT on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and 

(b) continue to (i) allocate 5% of the budget for the LDRRMF; (ii) utilize the Fund in accordance with the existing guidelines; and 

(c) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents.
continue to faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
continue to comply with regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.
continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

(a) Management   instruct the (i) officials involved in the processing  of   claims   to     be 
more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit; and (ii)  responsible and liable persons require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to comply with the revised documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish the regularity of transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal mayor require (a) the Municipal Accountant to provide the corresponding depreciation for all properties and equipment; and (b) the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Card for easy reconciliation of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MENRO (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste management  and activate the Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws especially on waste segregation at source; (b) continue to intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of the construction of MRF in every barangay and establishing programs on composting, recycling, re-use and recovery of solid waste generated; (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of ten barangays which remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003; and (d) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the (a) Local Sanggunian to pass an ordinance for the creation of Gad Code; and (b) instruct the GAD Focal Point System to coordinate with the Planning Department in the establishment of operational GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require (a) the Municipal Accountant to provide the corresponding depreciation for all depreciable properties and equipment; (b) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain PC, PPELC, and Real Property Card for easy reconciliation of the accounts; (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments are adequately and properly supported by required documents prior to payment; and (d) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to correct  the misclassification of the PPE accounts in the Budget and the Accounting records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) formulate detailed policy guidelines concurred by the SB, in the granting  of various types of financial assistance parallel to the provisions of the DSWD MC to ensure that only those qualified constituents in crisis situation are extended financial assistance; (b) ensure that all payments must be made by check issued directly under the name of the beneficiary or in the form of cash advance of the disbursing officer in the Treasury Office; and (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments are adequately and properly supported by required documents prior to payment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to remit to the source agencies or to the Bureau of the Treasury the remaining unexpended balance of the trust funds totaling to ₱142,841.40.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)  (a) adhere strictly  to the guidelines on  project identification and inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP)  of development projects and refrain from charging expenditures that do not partake the nature of capital investments or those not essential or relevant to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes pursuant  to DILG and DBM  JMC No. 2017-1; (b) strictly comply with the provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006 in the implementation of infrastructure projects by administration through Pakyaw Contracts; and (c) require the Municipal Engineering Office to (i) fast track the execution  of  the remaining projects included in the AIP for CY 2019; and (ii) properly supervise pre-procurement activities and monitor the project  implementation to avoid delay and attain on a timely basis  the desired objectives of the projects planned  during the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the LDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF certified correct by the Local Accountant and submit to the AT on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and (b) continue to (i) allocate 5% of the budget for the LDRRMF; (ii) utilize the Fund in accordance with the existing guidelines; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) and the Acting Municipal Environment and Resources Officer (MENRO) (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste management and activate the Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws especially on waste segregation at source;  (b) continue to intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of  the construction of MRF in every barangay and establishing programs on  composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated; (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to component barangays which  remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003; and (d) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal.

We recommended that Management (a) advise the member of the Local School Board (LSB) to refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities in which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; (b) maximize the utilization of SEF on relevant and necessary activities to attain its objectives; (c) instruct the District Supervisor to submit the lacking documents in the claim of food allowance; and (d) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents before effecting payments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) direct the Human Resources Department to recommend and plan an appropriate capacity building on GAD like trainings and seminars of key personnel of GFPS to ensure that they are effectively capacitated on GAD processes and mainstreaming of GAD PPAs; (b) instruct the Local Sanggunian to pass an ordinance for the creation of the Municipal GAD Code; (c) in coordination with the Municipal Planning Department, establish linkage of the existing Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) with the GAD sex-aggregated data to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; (d) utilize the fund in accordance with the guidelines on the matter; and (e) submit all the reports required under Magna Carta of Women (MCW).
We recommended that Management continue to faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the (a) Management   instruct the (i) officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit; and (ii)  responsible and liable persons require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory; and (b) Municipal Accountant to comply with the revised documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to establish the regularity of transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) comply strictly with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-022 dated February 10, 1997, specifically on the grant of additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated; and (b) increase the fidelity bond of the above mentioned accountable officers and employees to an amount sufficient to cover the maximum accountability that they handle for a given period in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Fidelity Bond provided by the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (a) coordinate with the Office of the Provincial Accountant to request a monthly report on the receivables of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGUs – PGB and the corresponding Tax Revenue accounts; and (b) request the timely remittance of the share of the Municipality on the Real property tax/Special education Tax collected by the PGB.

We recommend that Management (a) formulate detailed policy guidelines concurred by the SB, in the grant of various types of financial assistance parallel to the provisions of the DSWD MC to ensure that only those qualified constituents in crisis situation are extended financial assistance; (b) ensure that all payments must be made by check issued directly under the name of the beneficiary or in the form of cash advance of the disbursing officer in the Treasury Office; and (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments be adequately and properly supported by required documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to remit to the source agencies or to the Bureau of treasury the remaining unexpended balance of the trust funds totaling to ₱113,167.75.

We recommend that Management (a) require the Municipal Accountant to stop the grant of cash advances to school officials and give advances only to designated Disbursing Officers (DOs) of the Municipality; and (b) require the DepEd officials to submit the necessary supporting documents to support the liquidation of the above cash advances.

We recommend that Management through the Local School Board (a) give priority to programs, projects and activities enumerated in the Joint Circular in the preparation of the SEF budget and (b) stop the practice of improperly charging ineligible expenses to SEF which the DepEd has the mandate to disburse out of its own appropriation. 

We recommend that Management (a) refrain from purchasing supplies and materials thru reimbursement; and (b) ensure that all payments must be made by check issued directly under the name of the supplier.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) instruct the MDC and the key officials to adhere strictly with the guidelines on project identification and inclusion in the AIP of development projects and its utilization pursuant to DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1; (b) maximize the utilization of the 20% DF for projects that are in the nature of investment or capital expenditures in order to best serve the Municipality’s constituents; (c) require the Municipal Engineer to (i) ensure that the infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport, and (ii) respective contractors to repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to the one year warranty from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184; (d) professionalize the BAC Secretariat and TWG in the performance of their mandated functions and responsibilities specially in complying with PhilGEPS requirements; and (e) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects before payment is made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) MDRRMO to prepare and submit to the COA Auditor the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF; (b) incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the charges to the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF; (c) concerned officials to refrain from using the DRRMF for transactions which were not disaster-related; and (d) Municipal Accountant submit the deficient supporting documents.
We recommended that Management continue to faithfully comply with the regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management (a) continue to comply with the regulations on the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive  require the Municipal Accountant to (a) reconcile the Cash in Bank accounts with the depository bank and Treasurer’s records at least quarterly and conduct detailed analysis and review of the cash accounts; and (b) prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements for cash in bank accounts, and submit the same to the Audit Team for review and verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the Municipal Accountant to request for authority from the COA to write-off the dormant asset accounts pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2016-005 and 97-001.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive  require the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with the existing rules and regulations in order to prevent further deterioration of properties and generate additional funds for the Municipality.
observations.reason: The disposal of the unserviceable motor vehicles was not prioritized by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Human Resource Management Office to require the submission of an accomplishment report of job orders every month together with the approved DTR; and (b) install tighter internal control of its payroll funds  for job order employees by using the automated teller machine (ATM) payroll system.
observations.managementAction: a) Accomplishment reports were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit the required documents pursuant to the COA Circular No. 2012-001 and DILG MC  No. 
2014-155.

We recommended that Management (a) as head of the MDC, work on the identification of specific development projects to be funded by the 20% DF by ensuring that regular convention of the MDC is established to discuss, deliberate, and monitor the implementation of development projects under the Fund; (b) maximize the utilization of the 20% DF for projects that are in nature of investment or capital expenditures in order to best serve the Municipality’s constituents; and (c) henceforth, require the prior approval of the SB for generically described  projects in the AIP prior to their implementation in accordance with COA Memorandum 2010-014 dated April 22, 2010.

We recommended that the Management instruct the (a) Local Sanggunian to  spearhead the formulation of the GAD Code in collaboration with the LGU offices/departments through the Committee on Women and Family; and (b) GAD FPS to (i) coordinate with the Planning Department in the updating of the GAD  database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; (ii) specify the performance indicators and targets in the preparation of GAD Accomplishment Report; and (iii) submit all  the reports required under the MCW.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal  Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant refrain from charging inappropriate expenditures to the Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) comply strictly with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, specifically on the grant of additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated; and (b) increase the fidelity bond of the above mentioned accountable officers and employees to an amount sufficient to cover the maximum accountability that they handle for a given period in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Fidelity Bond provided by the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (a) coordinate with the Office of the Provincial Accountant to request a monthly report on the receivables of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from LGUs – PGB and the corresponding Tax Revenue accounts; and (b) request the timely remittance of the share of the Municipality on the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax collected by the PGB.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require (a) the Municipal Accountant to provide the corresponding depreciation for all properties and equipment; and (b) the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Card for easy reconciliation of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) formulate detailed policy guidelines concurred by the SB, on the grant of various types of financial assistance parallel to the provisions of the DSWD MC to ensure that only those qualified constituents in crisis situation are extended financial assistance; (b) ensure that all payments must be made by checks issued directly under the name of the beneficiary or in the form of cash advance of the disbursing officer in the Treasury Office; and (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments are adequately and properly supported by required documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to remit to the source agencies or to the Bureau of the Treasury the remaining unexpended balance of the trust funds totaling to ₱113,167.75.

We recommended that Management (a) require the Municipal Accountant to stop the grant of cash advances to school officials and give cash advances only to designated DOs of the Municipality; and (b) require the DepEd officials to submit the necessary supporting documents for the above cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Local School Board (a) give priority to programs, projects, and activities enumerated in the Joint Circular in the preparation of the SEF budget and (b) stop the practice of improperly charging ineligible expenses against the SEF which the DepEd has the mandate to disburse out of its own appropriation.

We recommended that Management (a) refrain from purchasing supplies and materials thru reimbursement; and (b) ensure that all payments must be made by check issued directly under the name of the supplier.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) instruct the MDC and key officials to adhere strictly with the guidelines on  project identification and inclusion in the AIP of development projects and its utilization pursuant  to DILG and DBM  JMC No. 2017-1; (b) maximize the utilization of the 20% DF for projects that are in the nature of investment or capital expenditures in order to best serve the Municipality’s constituents; (c) require the  Municipal Engineer to  i) ensure that the  infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport, and ii)  respective contractors to  repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to  the one year warranty from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184; (d) professionalize the BAC Secretariat and TWG in the performance of their mandated functions and responsibilities specially in complying with PhilGEPS requirements; and (e) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects  before payment is made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the a) MDRRMO to prepare and submit to the COA Auditor the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF; b) incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the charges to the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF; c) concerned officials to refrain from using the DRRMF for transactions which were not disaster-related; and d) Municipal Accountant submit the deficient supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MENRO (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste management and activate the Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws especially on waste segregation at source;  (b) continue to intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of  the construction of MRF in every barangay and establishing programs on  composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated; (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of the ten Barangays which  remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003; and (d) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the (a) Local Sanggunian to pass an ordinance for the creation of GAD Code; and (b) instruct the GAD Focal Point System to coordinate with the Planning Department in the establishment of operational GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation.
We recommended that Management continue to faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with the regulations on the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant to provide the corresponding depreciation for all properties and equipment; (b) require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE for further audit; (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property records for PPE and undertake regular reconciliation of their records; and (d) require the concerned Offices to exert their best efforts to retrieve the documents supporting the legal ownership of the untitled parcels of land.
observations.managementAction:
c) Property records were maintained for some properties.


d) Management exerted effort to retrieve the ownership documents of the untitled parcels of land; however, it was unsuccessful.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to request for authority from the COA to write-off the dormant asset accounts pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2016-005 and 97-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Accountant to provide the corresponding depreciation for all properties and equipment; (b) require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE for further audit; (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property records for PPE and undertake regular reconciliation of their records; and (d) require the concerned Offices to exert their best efforts to retrieve the documents supporting the legal ownership of the untitled parcels of land.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to undertake the immediate disposal of the unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with the existing rules and regulations in order to prevent further deterioration of properties and generate additional funds for the Municipality.

We recommended that Management (a) see to it that all applicable laws, rules, and regulations affecting cash advances and disbursement of funds are strictly adhered to; and (b) increase the fidelity bond of the above mentioned accountable officer and employees to an amount sufficient to cover the maximum accountability that they handle for a given period in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Fidelity Bond provided by the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to (a) submit the deficient documents; (b) ensure the submission to the Commission on Audit the cash advance voucher together with the transmittal and its supporting documents within seven (7) days the after release of the check; and (c) increase the fidelity bond of the above mentioned accountable officer to an amount sufficient to cover the maximum accountability that he handles for a given period in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Fidelity Bond provided by the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Human Resource Management Office to require the submission of an accomplishment report of job orders every month together with the approved DTR; and (b) install tighter internal control of its payroll funds for job order employees by using the automated teller machine (ATM) payroll system.

11.4. We recommended that Management (a) as head of the MDC, work on the identification of specific development projects to be funded by the 20% DF by ensuring that regular convention of the MDC is established to discuss, deliberate, and monitor the implementation of development projects under the Fund; (b) maximize the utilization of the 20% DF for projects that are in nature of investment or capital expenditures in order to best serve the Municipality’s constituents; and (c) henceforth, require the prior approval of the SB for generically described projects in the AIP prior to their implementation in accordance with COA Memorandum 2010-014 dated April 22, 2010.

We recommended that Management (a) transfer the remaining unexpended balance of the CY 2017 LDRRMF to the Trust Fund; (b) require the LDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Local Accountant and submitted to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; (c) continue to allocate 5% of the budget for the LDRRMF; (d) utilize the Fund in accordance with the existing guidelines; and (e) require the return of the subject vehicle by the District Supervisor to the MDRRM Office in support of its disaster risk reduction and management activities.

We recommended that the Management instruct the (a) Local Sanggunian to  spearhead the formulation of the GAD Code in collaboration with the LGU offices/departments through the Committee on Women and Family; and (b) GAD FPS to (i) coordinate with the Planning Department in the updating of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; (ii) specify the performance indicators and targets in the preparation of GAD Accomplishment Report; and (iii) submit all the reports required under the MCW.

We recommended that Management (a) instruct the LSB to (i) give priority to programs, projects and activities enumerated in the JC in the preparation of the SEF budget; and (ii) stop the practice of improperly charging inappropriate expenses to SEF which the DepEd has the mandate to disburse out of its own appropriation; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to demand the immediate liquidation or settlement of the financial assistance totaling ₱70,000.00 which were given to DepEd without legal basis.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste management and activate the Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws; (b) intensify the information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of  establishment of MRF in every barangay; and (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of the ten component Barangays who have remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant refrain from charging inappropriate expenditures to the Fund.
We recommended that Management continue to faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Accountant to make charges against  the GAD Fund to only  PPAs that are relevant and will address the identified gender issues and their causes; (b) ensure that the GAD  PPAs respond to the identified gender issues and concerns and that GAD mandates are implemented; (c) conduct gender analysis using the prescribed tools  to ensure that PPAs are gender responsive to  address the prevailing and recurring issues and concerns arising from the unequal status of women and men stakeholders, clients and constituents; and  (d) monitor the implementation of the GAD PPAs and prepare the AR in the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO and the LDRRM Council take the necessary and timely recommendation to the SB for the declaration of a state of calamity if warranted by the prevailing conditions while providing immediate response to disaster victims. In case there is a declaration of a state of calamity by the SB, the corresponding lifting under appropriate circumstances thereafter, shall also be issued.

We recommended that the MSWDO conduct actual validation of the families and individuals in the affected barangays to come up with the victims’ profiles containing updated and realistic data to become the basis in next year’s planning activities to ensure adequate and appropriate procurement of relief goods on a timely basis to properly address the needs of disaster victims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) establish a materials recovery facility in every barangay to regulate wastes collection at source and  reduce the volume of wastes at a manageable level  thus, minimize the cost of tipping fees; (b) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal; (c) provide the necessary protective personal equipment to all  personnel, whether LGU personnel  or hauler,  who are  directly involved in the operation of solid wastes management; (d) comply with the  Minimum Requirements for Establishing and Operating Transfer Stations and; (e) designate a MENRO to oversee and monitor the Municipality’s solid wastes management system and practices.

Likewise, proper reporting, accounting, and recording of accountability for donated goods should be made by the respective offices.

16.4	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the inclusion of a rescue vehicle in the AIP and instruct the MDRRMO and concerned officials to undertake the procurement of the needed rescue vehicle/equipment.

17.12  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) establish a materials recovery facility in every barangay to regulate wastes collection at source and  reduce the volume of wastes at a manageable level  thus, minimize the cost of tipping fees; (b) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal; (c) provide the necessary protective personal equipment to all  personnel, whether LGU personnel  or hauler,  who are  directly involved in the operation of solid wastes management; (d) comply with the  Minimum Requirements for Establishing and Operating Transfer Stations and; (e) designate a MENRO to oversee and monitor the Municipality’s solid wastes management system and practices.

18.4 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue to be compliant with GSIS regulations on the deduction of premium contributions and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
19.5   We recommended that the Municipality continue to be compliant with the provisions of BIR regulations on the remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Accountant:

initiate the conduct of reconciliation of the Cash- in- Bank records with the depository bank within ten (10) days from receipt of the Bank Statements and with the Treasurer’s cash records at least quarterly;
observations.managementAction:
The reconciliation process could not be made on a regular basis due to lack of Accounting personnel.

Augmentation of personnel could not be made due to limited funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management implement key controls over the  acquisition , storage and issuance of property assets by :

(a) forming an inventory committee that will conduct an annual count of all PPE items including the preparation and submission of the inventory reports; 

(b) instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain subsidiary records for PPE and undertake reconciliation of their records; and 

(c) direct all concerned Municipal officials to exert effort in the reproduction of titles on all lots owned by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Inventory count was not made at year-end due to lack of personnel and Management will consider additional personnel in next year’s budget.

Due to manpower constraints reconciliation was not done.

Management will consider in the next year’s budget, funding for personnel to do the work and cost of property titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) adopt the best environmental, economical, efficient and effective practice/s in ecological waste management; and  (b) immediately address the hazards posed by the maintenance of an open dumpsite by converting it to a controlled one.
observations.managementAction: Transfer Station was utilized since the closure of the dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to being compliant with the government’s agenda in promoting the welfare of women specifically on poverty alleviation, economic empowerment especially of marginalized women, protection, promotion and fulfillment of women’s human rights, and practice of gender-responsive governance.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that it has always been compliant with government’s regulations pertaining to GAD, the main reason why they were able to receive the Seal of Good Local Governance from the DILG in CY 20

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continuously comply with the prescribed provisions relative to LDRRMF in order to strengthen the disaster risk reduction and management system thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to devote more attention regarding this matter in 2016 to be able to comply with the audit recommendation and applicable recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality continue to being compliant with the deduction of GSIS premiums and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the Municipality continue to being compliant in the withholding of the appropriate taxes and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
